heading: Civilian clothing allowance
label: 419 Civilian clothing allowance
text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense, an officer of an armed force who is assigned to a permanent duty station at a location outside the United States may be paid a civilian clothing allowance in such amount as the Secretary shall determine under regulations if such officer is required to wear civilian clothing all or a substantial portion of the time in the performance of the officer’s official duties. A clothing allowance under this section is in addition to any uniform allowance to which an officer is otherwise entitled under this title.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 419 was renumbered .section 420 of this title

Amendments1989— substituted “an officer” for “a officer” in two places and struck out “to” after “may be paid”.Pub. L. 101–1891988— substituted “officer” for “member” in three places, “may be paid” for “is entitled”, and “officer’s” for “member’s”.Pub. L. 100–456

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–180, div. A, title VI, § 611(b)101 Stat. 1093Dec. 4, 1987“, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act []. No member may be paid a clothing allowance under such section for any period before such date.”Section 419 of title 37Dec. 4, 1987
